title: Silent speech interfaces
task: Silent Speech Interface (SSI) overview: technologies, applications, and challenges
key_contributions: Defines the SSI concept and surveys SSI technologies spanning multiple sensing modalities.
Summarizes common challenges in SSI (data, sensing variability, decoding, usability) and outlines future directions.
Serves as a canonical SSI reference for positioning EPG-based silent spelling within broader SSI research.
limitations: As a 2010 survey, it predates many recent deep-learning-based SSI advances.
Survey scope emphasizes representative systems rather than exhaustive coverage of every subcommunity.
reported_results_summary: Survey paper; it reviews representative SSI systems and challenges rather than reporting a single benchmark.
relevance_to_epgspeller: Provides a standard SSI definition and taxonomy to support clearer positioning/related work (W1/W6). Also motivates why comparing open-vocabulary vs constrained decoding must be protocol-aware (W2).
takeaways_for_related_work_section: A canonical SSI survey defining problem scope and modality taxonomy; can be cited to contextualize EPGSpeller as an articulatory-sensing SSI focused on open-vocabulary silent spelling.

sources.title: KITopen: Silent Speech Interfaces (Denby et al., Speech Communication 2010)

uncertain: This JSON does not enumerate the full list of modality-specific systems reviewed; consult the paper for details.
